authoritative_sources.title: Shortening the Securities Transaction Settlement Cycle
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SEC guidance is authoritative for the U.S. T+1 settlement baseline, completion of allocations, confirmations, and affirmations by trade date, and investment-adviser recordkeeping tied to those events.
used_for: trade-date allocation, confirmation, and affirmation expectations
investment-adviser records for confirmations, allocations, and affirmations
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains lifecycle timing, minimum evidence expectations, and refusal when trade-date workflow artifacts are absent.

authoritative_sources.title: Risk Alert: Division of Examinations T+1 Settlement Cycle
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SEC examinations guidance is authoritative for operational risk and control expectations around allocations, confirmations, affirmations, and settlement readiness under T+1.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains exception handling, evidence capture, and escalation when same-day processing or settlement-readiness evidence is weak.

authoritative_sources.title: FINRA Rule 11870 - Customer Account Transfer Contracts
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FINRA Rule 11870 is the primary public rulebook source for broker-dealer transfer timing, receiving-versus-delivering responsibilities, and transfer exception handling.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains custody-transfer workflows so the lane requests authoritative transfer records instead of inferring completion from narrative updates.

authoritative_sources.title: CTM - Match to Instruct Workflow
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DTCC operates the institutional trade-processing infrastructure referenced by market participants for public CTM workflow semantics, including the linkage between matching, affirmation, and instruction delivery.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Allocation, confirmation, affirmation, and instruction enrichment
used_for: automatic instruction delivery after central match
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Defines realistic investment-operations terminology and the evidence chain between matched allocations, affirmations, and settlement instructions.

authoritative_sources.title: DTCC's ALERT Surpasses 15 Million Standing Settlement Instructions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DTCC is authoritative for ALERT workflow semantics because ALERT is the industry SSI utility used to maintain and communicate standing settlement instructions and reduce settlement failures.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: SSI validation and settlement instruction control
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains the lane to treat SSI validation as a named prerequisite instead of a generic data check.

authoritative_sources.title: Automated Customer Account Transfer Service (ACATS)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DTCC operates ACATS and is authoritative for public transfer-system statuses and message handling used by custody and wealth operations teams.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Defines realistic transfer artifact expectations and supports refusal of unsupported transfer-completion claims.

authoritative_sources.title: Services for Processing Corporate Action Events
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DTCC asset-services workflow guidance is authoritative for public corporate-action announcement, instruction, entitlement, allocation, and reporting lifecycle semantics used by securities operations teams.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains the lane to name corporate-action and income-processing artifacts explicitly rather than collapsing them into generic back-office work.

authoritative_sources.title: FINRA Rule 2232 - Customer Confirmations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FINRA Rule 2232 is an authoritative confirmation and disclosure rule that defines what trade-confirmation evidence must exist at or before completion of many customer transactions.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains completion-evidence handling and prevents the lane from treating unconfirmed customer activity as closed.

authoritative_sources.title: FINRA Rule 4511 - General Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FINRA Rule 4511 is a core books-and-records control baseline governing creation, preservation, and retrievability of operational evidence.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Defines what evidence must exist before a completion record or operations summary can be treated as supportable.

authoritative_sources.title: 17 CFR 240.15c3-3 - Customer Protection; Reserves and Custody of Securities
authoritative_sources.publisher: Electronic Code of Federal Regulations / U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Rule 15c3-3 is the primary custody-control baseline for possession, control, and safeguarding of customer securities held by broker-dealers.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains when the lane can treat custody movement, release, or settlement completion as supported by the visible evidence.

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and prerequisite verification
workflow_stages.description: Validate trade identity, account authority, allocations, SSIs, custody mappings, corporate-action source notices, and tenant-specific approvals before the lane progresses work.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Allocation, confirmation, affirmation, and instruction enrichment
workflow_stages.description: Move trades through allocation, confirmation, and affirmation readiness while tying instruction delivery and SSI accuracy to named workflow records.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Settlement, custody, and transfer progression
workflow_stages.description: Track settlement states, transfer statuses, custody holds, and fail conditions without claiming that unresolved breaks are complete.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Corporate-action, income, and entitlement processing
workflow_stages.description: Handle event announcements, entitlements, payment or instruction processing, and exceptions using named asset-servicing records and deadlines.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Reconciliation, completion evidence, and audit-ready closure
workflow_stages.description: Produce completion or fulfillment records only when underlying trade, settlement, custody, event, and ledger evidence supports closure.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: Trade blotters, order records, allocations, confirmations, and affirmations
Standing settlement instructions, custody instructions, depository messages, and account registration records
ACATS transfer forms, transfer statuses, and receiving-versus-delivering firm records
Corporate-action announcements, election notices, entitlement records, dividend or interest allocation details, and payment dates
Settlement status messages, fail reports, position statements, cash and position break logs, and general-ledger tie-outs
Approval histories, mandate records, restrictions, servicing notes, and client or counterparty communications
Case-management entries, document images, and exception queue records
systems_of_record: Order management, execution management, and trade-blotter systems
Institutional trade processing and affirmation systems such as CTM and related matching platforms
Standing settlement instruction and custodian reference systems such as ALERT or tenant SSI repositories
Custody, depository, and transfer platforms including ACATS-connected systems
Case-management, CRM, and document management systems
Compliance, fraud, and escalation systems when risk posture changes

allowed: Determine whether available trade, custody, transfer, settlement, and corporate-action records support progression, hold, rework, or completion within investment-operations workflows.
Produce execution summaries, exception notes, completion records, and escalation packages tied to named systems-of-record evidence.
Identify when post-trade, entitlement, or reconciliation blockers require the next operational owner.
escalate: A legal, tax, portfolio, or policy interpretation materially changes whether the request may proceed.
Custody, depository, transfer, corporate-action, or ledger records conflict and need human review or adjacent-owner resolution.
Approval authority, client impact, or suspicious behavior changes the risk posture beyond operational handling.
refuse: The request asks for investment advice, trade selection, portfolio rebalancing, legal interpretation, or final compliance signoff outside the lane.
The request asks to approve a disbursement, override a hold, waive a market cutoff, or mark work complete without authoritative evidence.
Required tenant facts, account authority, or systems-of-record context are missing and would change the outcome.

domain_failure_modes: Advancing a trade or transfer without documented allocations, affirmations, or standing settlement instructions required to support settlement.
Claiming completion while settlement, custody, entitlement, or ledger records still show unresolved breaks or open exceptions.
Treating CTM, custodian, ACATS, corporate-action, and internal ledger statuses as equivalent when they conflict materially.
Allowing urgency or client pressure to override cutoffs, custody holds, approval thresholds, or named event deadlines.
Collapsing corporate-action and income-processing work into tax, legal, or portfolio recommendations.
Using the industry name cosmetically and answering with generic finance process language that omits investment-operations-specific records and systems.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion
Normal in-scope execution with realistic terminology
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Cross-border cutoffs and local market settlement conventions
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Local market deadlines, custodian cutoffs, and affirmation timing can vary by jurisdiction and asset class. The pack therefore requires tenant-supplied market and custodian context before authorizing high-impact progression.
